page.title: SSH Identity & Servers
page.subtitle: Configure your local identity and manage your remote connections securely.
step0.label: STEP 0 · OPTIONAL
step0.title: SSH Server
step0.subtitle: Turn this machine into an SSH host. Other devices on your network can then connect to it. Skip if you only need to connect to remote servers.
enable.title: Enable SSH on This Machine
enable.desc: Starts the SSH daemon and opens port 22 in the firewall (ufw / firewalld). Requires passwordless sudo or polkit access.
step1.label: STEP 1 · REQUIRED
step1.title: Local Identity Setup
step1.subtitle: Your SSH key is your identity on the network. Generate one here, then copy the public key to any remote server or GitHub/GitLab account you want to access without a password.
generate.title: Generate Local ID
generate.desc: Create a secure SSH key to identify this machine to remote servers.
identity.title: Identity Options
identity.desc: Copy your public key for manual setup, or use the automatic "Enable Remote Access" button after connecting.
step2.label: STEP 2 · CONNECT
step2.title: Saved Servers
step2.subtitle: Bookmark remote machines by label. Hit Connect to open a live shell, browse files, or push your public key automatically.
bookmark.label: Label
activity.title: Connection History
activity.subtitle: Recent connections and active sessions. Click a session to re-open the terminal or file browser.
activity.empty: No connections yet. Click "Connect" on a saved server.
prereqs.title: 📋 Remote Device Prerequisites
prereqs.desc: The remote machine must have SSH running and port 22 open before you can connect. Run the commands matching its distro:
ft.selectFiles: Please select files to upload first.
ft.selectRemoteDest: Please select a remote destination folder.
ft.selectRemoteSource: Please select a remote source file/folder.
terminal.title: SSH Session: {{name}}
terminal.hint: Type <mono>exit</mono> to end the session — window closes automatically
password.title: 🔑 Activate Remote Access
password.desc: Enter the password for {{user}}@{{host}} to enable the file browser.
password.noKey: No SSH key found. Generate one in the Wizard first.
flatpakHint: Flatpak note: if SSH key/files are inaccessible, grant access to `~/.ssh` for the app (for example with `flatpak override --user --filesystem=~/.ssh io.github.karimodora.LinuxDevHome`).
